title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After defeating a Red Hand Party member, Anthony retreats to his room to read a memoir belonging to an old count. He orders dinner, observing that the waiter, an Italian man named Gisippo, appears intensely interested in the manuscripts on his table. Anthony finds the memoir's handwriting difficult to read and, after finishing the fourth chapter, decides to sleep. He locks his letters and the memoir's packaging in a suitcase, places a chair and a water bottle against his door as a defense, and hides the memoir under his pillow.

Four hours later, Anthony is awakened by a shadow near his window. Gisippo enters through the window and attacks Anthony with a long knife. During a struggle, Gisippo chokes Anthony, but the knife falls to the ground. Anthony attempts to block the door, but realizes Gisippo entered via the window; the intruder escapes by jumping to a neighboring balcony. While Anthony manages to retrieve the memoir from under his pillow, he discovers that Gisippo has stolen the letters from his suitcase.

Elsewhere, Virginia Rue returns to her residence on Pound Street. Her butler, Chifley, informs her that a foreign man is in the study claiming to have important information regarding her late husband, Major Timothy Rue. Virginia meets the man, a tall foreigner of flexible build, in the study. After a tense silence, the man presents Virginia with a letter, prompting her to examine whom it is addressed to.
